title: Cranford
author: Gaskell, Elizabeth Cleghorn
summary: Set in the small English town of Cranford, Elizabeth Gaskell's narrative describes a community dominated by women whose strict rules of visiting, economy, and decorum mask private hardships. The narrator records social rituals—tea-parties, minute calling hours, and concealed poverty—alongside eccentric characters: the proud but impoverished, the stingy yet genteel, and Captain Brown, a half-pay officer whose frank practicality and masculine common sense gradually wins the ladies’ respect. Episodes range from the rescue of Miss Betsy Barker’s Alderney cow to debates over fashion and propriety, painting a humorous, affectionate portrait of provincial female society and its quiet virtues and domestic kindness.

characters.name: Narrator
characters.personality: observant, conversational, affectionate reporter of Cranford society
characters.lifespan: Remains the storyteller who returns to and records Cranford's events in the narrative

characters.name: Captain Brown
characters.personality: wiry, practical, frank, manly common sense
characters.lifespan: Settles at Cranford with his daughters and wins respect through pragmatic counsel

characters.name: Miss Brown (eldest)
characters.age: about 40 (described as must have been forty)
characters.personality: sickly, pained, careworn, plain and hard-featured
characters.lifespan: Remains in Cranford; portrayed as careworn and domestic in the excerpt

characters.name: Miss Jessie Brown
characters.age: about 30 (described as ten years younger than her sister)
characters.personality: round-faced, dimpled, childlike, pretty and wondering
characters.lifespan: Remains in Cranford; noted for her childlike appearance and gentle nature

characters.name: Miss Betsy Barker
characters.personality: affectionate toward her Alderney cow, easily distressed about it
characters.lifespan: Her cow falls into a lime-pit but is rescued and she recovers from the alarm

characters.name: The Alderney (cow)
characters.personality: much-loved bovine, described as intelligent and remarkable
characters.lifespan: Tumbles into a lime-pit, is rescued, dressed in flannel, and returns to pasture

characters.name: Mrs Forrester
characters.personality: gentle, conceals limited means while acting hostess
characters.lifespan: Hosts parties while hiding her poverty by personally assisting with refreshments

characters.name: Honourable Mrs Jamieson
characters.personality: practices 'elegant economy', genteel though sparing with entertainments
characters.lifespan: Noted in society for modest entertainments despite rank as sister-in-law to an earl

characters.name: Miss Tyler
characters.lifespan: Referred to posthumously as a model of cleanliness and propriety

characters.name: Miss Jenkyns
characters.lifespan: Appears as a critic within Cranford society; remains an active voice in town talk

characters.name: The Surgeon
characters.personality: professional, makes rounds over large district
characters.lifespan: Keeps his round of thirty miles and sleeps at Cranford; otherwise unnamed

characters.name: Charity-school maiden (servant)
characters.personality: dutiful but physically slight, assists at entertainments
characters.lifespan: Performs household service for poor gentlefolk and aids in serving at parties

characters.name: Little boys (group)
characters.lifespan: Local youngsters who mock and mob novel sights like the red silk umbrella

characters.name: The Ladies of Cranford (collective)
characters.personality: strict in manners, proud, eccentric, kind and dictatorial in charity
characters.lifespan: Maintain town customs, visiting rules, and a shared genteel economy throughout the narrative

locations.name: Tinwald Mount (Tynwald Hill, Isle of Man)
Alderney (Island, source of Alderney cow name)
